logbook.aiInput: Describe your entry... (e.g. 'Lunch: pasta, 60g carbs…' or 'On Mar 15 I felt very sad' — a date in the text books to that day)

logbook.aiNoData: I couldn't identify any data. Please describe it more precisely or enter it manually.

stats.signInHint: Sign in to see your Buddy stats.

mood.empty: Start a conversation to track how your chats feel over time.

summaryView.summaryUnavailable: No reflection is available for this conversation yet.

buddy.title: Diabetes Buddy
buddy.intro: I'm your Diabetes Buddy. Here you can openly talk about your everyday life with diabetes – about frustrations, achievements, and everything that's on your mind. I'm not a therapist and not a doctor. My answers may contain errors.
buddy.aiTransparencyNotice: You are talking to an AI chatbot (GPT-4o-mini). Replies are generated automatically and are not reviewed by a human. The Buddy is not a therapist or a doctor. If you are in acute distress, please contact a professional counselling service.
buddy.suggestion1: I want to share a success
buddy.suggestion2: I feel overwhelmed by life with diabetes
buddy.defaultResponse: That's an interesting question. As a research prototype, I can't generate real AI answers yet, but in the finished version I would give you a helpful answer here. Is there anything else you'd like to discuss?
buddy.emotionalResponse: I hear that this is weighing on you. It's completely understandable to sometimes feel overwhelmed. Would you like to talk about what helps you in such moments?
buddy.dataResponse: It sounds like you mentioned data that I could suggest as a logbook entry. This feature is coming in a later version!
buddy.historyLoadFailed: Conversation history could not be loaded.

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: Glucose, mood, and a short AI summary for the period you select.
insights.tirBandHint: Values in the green segment: 70–180 mg/dL (time in range).
insights.noGlucoseInPeriod: No glucose readings in this period
insights.moodGlucoseSubtitle: Daily averages: glucose from the logbook; mood from logbook entries and ended buddy chats (emotions mapped to 1–5).
insights.chartNoData: Not enough data for this period yet.
insights.correlationFallback: Add more mood and glucose data to spot patterns.
insights.discussWithTeam: Discuss patterns with your healthcare team.
insights.motivationQuote: Every day with diabetes is a day where you care for yourself. You deserve recognition.
insights.goal1Title: This week, pay attention to how you feel after meals
insights.goal1Description: Quickly note how you feel right after eating.
insights.goal2Title: Every evening, write a short reflection entry
insights.goal2Description: Write 1–2 sentences: what went well and what's on your mind.
insights.insufficientData: Collect more data to receive personalized insights. Have a few conversations with your Diabetes Buddy and track your values.

import.title: Import Data
import.subtitle: Import data from external sources
import.info: You can import CSV files with a header row. Columns are auto-detected by name; adjust the mapping if needed.
import.importHelpNote: Date and time are interpreted in your local timezone. One row may create multiple entries (e.g. glucose, insulin, carbs). Empty or invalid fields are skipped.
import.dropzone: Drag CSV file here or click to select
import.skippedHint: Skipped rows had no valid date or no usable values, or the database rejected an insert.
import.parseError: The CSV could not be parsed. Check the header row and delimiter (comma or semicolon).
import.mappingInvalidDesc: Map at least one date or timestamp column (date alone is OK; date + time are combined automatically).
import.nothingToImportDesc: No row had a valid date and at least one glucose, insulin, or carb value.
import.unauthorizedImport: Please sign in to import data.
import.importError: Data could not be saved. Please try again later.
import.noColumnsParsed: Could not read the first row as a table header. Please use a CSV file with a header row.

auth.consentText:
This app is a research prototype for a scientific study at Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität Munich. Before you start, a few important points:

Your data: Your entries (blood glucose, insulin, meals, mood) and conversations with the Diabetes Buddy are stored pseudonymously in a database (EU server, Frankfurt).

AI processing: The chatbot and text analysis use OpenAI (GPT-4o-mini). Your messages are sent to OpenAI servers in the United States for this purpose—without your name or user ID.

Not a medical device: This app does not replace medical or therapeutic advice. AI answers may contain errors.

Voluntary participation: You can stop at any time and request deletion of your data (email: lilia@schraut.de).

Deletion: All data will be deleted after the study ends.
auth.privacyPolicyFull:
GlucoCompanion – research prototype for diabetes self-management
As of: March 2026

1. Controller

The controller within the meaning of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is:
Lilia Schraut
Email: lilia@schraut.de

Data processing takes place in the context of a bachelor’s thesis at Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität Munich.

2. Purpose of processing

GlucoCompanion is a research prototype developed as part of a scientific thesis. The app is used to explore how AI-supported features can help emotional and metabolic self-management for people with diabetes.

The app is not a medical device and does not replace medical or therapeutic advice.

The data collected are used solely for scientific analysis in the context of the bachelor’s thesis. There is no commercial use.

3. Legal basis

Processing is based on your explicit consent pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR in conjunction with Art. 9(2)(a) GDPR (consent to processing special categories of personal data, here: health data). You may withdraw your consent at any time with effect for the future.

4. What data are collected?

4.1 Registration data
• Freely chosen pseudonym (no real name required)
• PIN (stored as a cryptographic hash via bcrypt – the PIN itself is not stored)
• No email address, name, or phone number

4.2 Health-related data (logbook)
• Blood glucose values (mg/dL)
• Insulin doses (units, insulin type)
• Meals (description, estimated carbohydrates)
• Physical activity
• Mood entries (free text and/or scale value 1–5)
• Timestamps of all entries

4.3 Conversation data (Diabetes Buddy)
• Your messages to the AI chatbot
• The AI chatbot’s replies
• AI-generated conversation summaries
• AI-generated emotion analyses (6 dimensions: joy, surprise, sadness, anger, fear, disgust)

4.4 Technical data
• Pseudonymised user ID (UUID)
• Session cookies for authentication (httpOnly, no tracking cookies)

5. Use of artificial intelligence

The app uses two AI features based on OpenAI’s GPT-4o-mini language model:

5.1 Diabetes Buddy (chatbot)
The Diabetes Buddy is an AI-supported chatbot for emotional self-management. Conversations follow cognitive-behavioural (CBT) approaches. The Buddy is not a therapist, not a doctor, and not a medical device. Its answers may be wrong and do not replace professional advice.

5.2 Logbook extraction
The extraction feature turns free-text logbook input into structured entries (e.g. glucose values, meals). The AI estimates carbohydrate values using general nutrition knowledge. These estimates are indicative only and do not replace professional dietary advice.

5.3 Transparency notice under the EU AI Act
Under the EU Artificial Intelligence Act, we inform you that you interact with an AI system in this app. Chatbot replies and text extractions are generated automatically and are not reviewed by a human. The AI features are for research purposes only.

6. Transfers to third parties

To provide the app, data are transferred to the following providers:

Supabase
• Purpose: Database (PostgreSQL)
• Data: All user data (pseudonymised)
• Server location: EU (Frankfurt)

OpenAI
• Purpose: AI processing (chat, extraction, summaries)
• Data: Text messages, logbook entries (without user ID)
• Server location: USA

Vercel
• Purpose: Hosting the web app
• Data: HTTP requests, IP addresses (server logs)
• Server location: USA

Note on transfers to third countries: Transfers to OpenAI and Vercel (USA) are based on your consent (Art. 49(1)(a) GDPR) and the EU standard contractual clauses provided by those vendors. US authorities may theoretically access such data. Your data are transferred in pseudonymised form – OpenAI does not receive your user ID or other identifying information.

7. Data security
• Pseudonymisation: Access is only via a chosen pseudonym and PIN. No real names or email addresses are collected.
• Encryption: All transfers use HTTPS/TLS. The PIN is stored as a bcrypt hash.
• Access control: App access is restricted by an access code issued only to study participants.
• Data isolation: Each user sees only their own data; separation is enforced on the server.

8. Retention and deletion

Your data are stored for the duration of the study and until submission of the bachelor’s thesis. After completion, all personal data are fully deleted, including logbook entries, conversations, summaries, and user accounts.

Only anonymised, aggregated analyses are used in the thesis. Individual user profiles or conversation histories are not published.

9. Your rights

You have the following rights regarding your personal data:
• Withdraw consent (Art. 7(3) GDPR): at any time by email to lilia@schraut.de
• Access (Art. 15 GDPR): you may request information about stored data
• Erasure (Art. 17 GDPR): you may request immediate deletion of your data
• Rectification (Art. 16 GDPR): you may request correction of inaccurate data
• Data portability (Art. 20 GDPR): you may request your data in a machine-readable format
• Complaint to a supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR): you may lodge a complaint with the competent data protection authority.
